strategy: Solo-friendly (7,000 health). He mirrors his own casts into the opposite school and can steal your blades, so protect your key blade with Aegis.
He only resists Fire and Ice 15% and has no boost weakness, so hit with any off-Fire/Ice school you like.
Do not over-stack blades he can steal. Keep one Aegis-protected blade and a Feint, then hit.
He is stunable, so stun to skip a turn and deny his cheat-casts.
Enter on time (late entry costs you pips) and finish with one strong hit.
cheatNotes: Joining after round 2 gets you hit with a Balance Steal Pips.
When he casts Fireblade or Iceblade he sometimes also cheat-casts the opposite blade.
That blade cheat can chain several times in a row.
When he casts certain attacks he sometimes also cheat-casts the opposite-school version.
When he casts his spells he sometimes cheat-casts the opposite-school version.
He may also steal your blades.
